source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Rubric for Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.pdf
output_file: 4-5.FL.2 Rubric for Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.md

analysis.summary: This is an assessment rubric for evaluating student performance on SMART goal brainstorming and planning in a grades 4-5 financial literacy curriculum. The rubric measures five criteria (Goals, Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-based) across four performance levels (Excellent, Satisfactory, Needs Improvement, Incomplete).

entities.name: Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy
entities.description: Elementary level financial literacy curriculum for students in grades 4 and 5

entities.name: Lesson 2
entities.description: Second lesson in the financial literacy curriculum sequence

entities.name: Handout B: SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning
entities.description: Student worksheet or handout focused on SMART goal development

entities.name: SMART Goals
entities.description: Goal-setting framework using Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-based criteria

entities.name: Specific Criteria
entities.description: Ability to define goals with clear, specific details

entities.name: Measurable Criteria
entities.description: Ability to include clear criteria for measuring progress in goals

entities.name: Achievable Criteria
entities.description: Ability to set realistic and attainable goals

entities.name: Relevant Criteria
entities.description: Ability to create goals that are relevant and meaningful

entities.name: Time-based Criteria
entities.description: Ability to include clear timeframes for goal completion

entities.name: Assessment Rubric
entities.description: Evaluation tool with four performance levels: Excellent, Satisfactory, Needs Improvement, Incomplete
